System Documentation

This document serves as the current end-to-end technical record for the repository. It consolidates source preparation, GAN-based dataset extension, train-only forecasting experiments, frozen deployed models, and the outputs exposed through the dashboard and API for both gold and silver.

System-level interpretation. Within this project, GANs function as scenario-generation and dataset-extension mechanisms rather than as the final forecasting models themselves. Their role is to construct future-dated multivariate panels on which downstream CNN-BiLSTM forecasters may be evaluated over longer horizons. Forecasting retrains are subsequently executed on train-only CSVs, compared across seeds and experiment variants, and only the selected frozen artifacts are connected to the API.

Rationale For GAN-Based Extension

  • The forecasting models consume interacting market and macroeconomic variables rather than a single univariate target.
  • The forecasting workflow operates in return space, implying that useful synthetic data must preserve return distributions and dependence structure instead of merely producing visually smooth price levels.
  • Simple interpolation is generally too smooth for financial time series and does not preserve multivariate joint behaviour.
  • Accordingly, GANs are used here as multivariate future-panel generators for augmentation, synthetic holdout construction, and dashboard simulation.
Methodological boundary. Strong synthetic-data fidelity does not, by itself, establish real-market forecasting skill. In the sections that follow, any evaluation rows marked as GAN-generated should be interpreted as synthetic actuals and therefore support internal validation and simulation analysis rather than real-market performance claims.

System Flow

  1. Observed source data is cleaned and aligned into a prepared business-day panel for each asset.
  2. GAN validation is then used to identify a future extension candidate that best preserves return-space behaviour.
  3. The selected extension is appended to construct the extended train and test CSVs.
  4. Forecast retraining experiments operate on the train CSVs only, using Optuna together with multi-seed CNN-BiLSTM refits.
  5. Only selected frozen model artifacts are deployed into api.py; the dashboard therefore reads precomputed predictions rather than retraining online.
  6. The test CSVs remain outside the train-only retraining loops and serve as the dashboard's walk-forward simulation window.

Cross-Asset Summary

This section documents the currently selected GAN dataset df_gold_dataset_gepu together with the frozen forecasting model associated with the gold pipeline. The presentation distinguishes between the observed historical sample, the GAN-generated extension, the subset admitted to forecast training, and the rows reserved for prediction-only evaluation.

Start-To-End Workflow

  1. The process begins with the observed multivariate source panel, which is transformed into a business-day-aligned and fully imputed dataset suitable for generative modelling.
  2. Multiple GAN seeds and candidate continuations are then estimated and assessed with respect to return-distribution fidelity, autocorrelation preservation, and cross-feature dependence structure.
  3. The selected continuation is appended to the observed history, thereby producing the extended dataset used in downstream forecasting experiments.
  4. CNN-BiLSTM forecasting models are trained exclusively on the designated train CSV; the dashboard test CSV is excluded from the train-only retraining studies documented here.
  5. The currently deployed frozen forecaster is loaded from models\gold_train_only_retrained\seed_99, after which predictions are precomputed against the untouched dashboard test CSV.
  6. These frozen predictions are subsequently exposed through the API and dashboard, such that date advancement traverses a precomputed prediction tape rather than initiating online retraining.

Dataset Lifecycle

Stage File Rows Start date End date Notes
Raw source dataset df_gold_dataset_gepu.csv 3898 2015-03-31 2025-11-30 Original source panel used by the GAN pipeline.
Prepared GAN-ready dataset df_gold_dataset_gepu_gan_ready.csv 2782 2015-03-31 2025-11-26 Business-day or cleaned panel used directly by the GAN.
Extended dataset df_gold_dataset_gepu_extended.csv 3068 2015-03-31 2026-12-31 Prepared history plus GAN-generated future rows.
Forecast train split df_gold_dataset_gepu_extended_train.csv 2878 2015-03-31 2026-04-09 Rows available to the frozen forecasting model.
Forecast evaluation window derived from extended dataset 190 2026-04-10 2026-12-31 Rows used for actual-vs-prediction evaluation in this report.

What Was Used For Forecast Training And What Was Not

Partition Rows Start date End date Meaning
Observed rows used in forecast training 2782 2015-03-31 2025-11-26 Observed price-level rows from the prepared/source dataset that were available to the frozen forecaster.
GAN-generated rows used in forecast training 96 2025-11-27 2026-04-09 Synthetic rows appended before the forecasting train cutoff.
Observed rows not used in forecast training 0 n/a n/a Held-out observed rows used only for prediction/evaluation, not for fitting the frozen forecaster.
GAN-generated rows not used in forecast training 190 2026-04-10 2026-12-31 Held-out synthetic rows used only for prediction/evaluation, not for fitting the frozen forecaster.
Model partition Rows First model date Last model date Notes
Forecast-train model rows 2876 2015-04-01 2026-04-08 These are the actual supervised rows after pct_change() and the t+1 target shift.
Forecast-eval model rows 188 2026-04-13 2026-12-30 These rows were not used for fitting; they are forecast contexts/targets only.

Preparation Notes

  • Prepared on a business-day calendar from the first to last observed target date.
  • Weekend rows carrying Gold_Futures were shifted to the prior business day before collapsing duplicates.
  • Numeric columns were filled with time interpolation followed by forward/back fill.

Interpretation And Validity

  • The extended file is structurally valid: no duplicate dates, no null cells, and the generated horizon reaches the intended end date.
  • The current selected GAN candidate is rated good and clears the repo gate with avg_ks_stat=0.0850, avg_acf_gap=0.0530, and corr_gap=0.0227.
  • The forecasting model predictions shown below are produced on rows that were not used to fit the frozen forecasting model.
  • This makes the report valid for internal model-behavior analysis, dataset-extension validation, and simulation testing.
  • The key limitation is that any evaluation rows marked as GAN-generated are synthetic actuals, so they support internal validation rather than real-market performance claims.

This section documents the currently selected GAN dataset silver_RRL_interpolate together with the frozen forecasting model associated with the silver pipeline. The presentation distinguishes between the observed historical sample, the GAN-generated extension, the subset admitted to forecast training, and the rows reserved for prediction-only evaluation.

Start-To-End Workflow

  1. The process begins with the observed multivariate source panel, which is transformed into a business-day-aligned and fully imputed dataset suitable for generative modelling.
  2. Multiple GAN seeds and candidate continuations are then estimated and assessed with respect to return-distribution fidelity, autocorrelation preservation, and cross-feature dependence structure.
  3. The selected continuation is appended to the observed history, thereby producing the extended dataset used in downstream forecasting experiments.
  4. CNN-BiLSTM forecasting models are trained exclusively on the designated train CSV; the dashboard test CSV is excluded from the train-only retraining studies documented here.
  5. The currently deployed frozen forecaster is loaded from models\silver_RRL_interpolate\seed_42, after which predictions are precomputed against the untouched dashboard test CSV.
  6. These frozen predictions are subsequently exposed through the API and dashboard, such that date advancement traverses a precomputed prediction tape rather than initiating online retraining.

Interpretation And Validity

  • The extended file is structurally valid: no duplicate dates, no null cells, and the generated horizon reaches the intended end date.
  • The current selected GAN candidate is rated good and clears the repo gate with avg_ks_stat=0.0799, avg_acf_gap=0.0427, and corr_gap=0.0234.
  • The forecasting model predictions shown below are produced on rows that were not used to fit the frozen forecasting model.
  • This makes the report valid for internal model-behavior analysis, dataset-extension validation, and simulation testing.
  • The key limitation is that any evaluation rows marked as GAN-generated are synthetic actuals, so they support internal validation rather than real-market performance claims.

References

  • Rama Cont (2001), Empirical properties of asset returns: stylized facts and statistical issues - Motivates evaluating synthetic financial data in return space and preserving dependence structure.
  • Jinsung Yoon, Daniel Jarrett, Mihaela van der Schaar (2019), Time-series Generative Adversarial Networks - Supports GAN-based sequence generation when temporal dynamics matter.
  • Magnus Wiese et al. (2020), Quant GANs: Deep Generation of Financial Time Series - Direct financial-time-series support for GANs that preserve volatility structure and dependence.
  • Justin Hellermann et al. (2022), Financial Time Series Data Augmentation with Generative Adversarial Networks and Extended Intertemporal Return Plots - Supports GAN-generated financial data as augmentation for downstream forecasting experiments.
